mappings.rationale: Directly covers Australian history including British colonization, self-government, federation, the commonwealth, and White Australia policy discussed in subsection B.
Relevant to British colonial settlement and governance in Australia, New Zealand, and across Oceania discussed throughout this section.
Directly relevant to British colonization of Australia and New Zealand, colonial government, and the establishment of the Australian commonwealth within the British Empire.
Relevant to the effects of colonization on indigenous peoples of Oceania and the colonial-settler dynamics discussed in subsections A.3, B.4, and C.2.
Relevant to the European exploration of Australia, Melanesia, Micronesia, and Polynesia discussed in subsections A.3 and B.1.
Relevant to the federation movement in Australia (1860-1901), the development of national identity, and New Zealand's path to self-government.
Relevant to the White Australia immigration policy and the patterns of British colonial migration discussed in subsections B.2 and B.3.
Relevant to Australia's cooperation with Britain in World War I discussed in subsection B.3.
Relevant to the prehistory and ethnography of Australia, Melanesia, Micronesia, and Polynesia discussed in subsection A.2.
Relevant to the ethnography and diverse peoples of Oceania, Aboriginal populations, and Maori people discussed in subsections A.2, B.4, and C.2.
